title: The One With The Baby On The Bus

jokes.text: Who the winny-binny boy? Oh, you the winny-winny-binny-binny boy!
jokes.setup: Someone trying to soothe a crying baby
jokes.punchline: Absurd baby talk escalation with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Generic baby talk humor, relies on silly sounds rather than clever construction

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, what's wrong, sweetie? Goo goo ga ga?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Is it your diaper? Your tummy? Boop boop snoozle?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, let's try this: Wiggle wiggle flibberdibbet zoompity zoop!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Shnicka-shnicka-boom boom, little guy? Sproingy-doingy-dip dip dip?

jokes.text: Ben crying stops when passed to different person, immediately resumes when passed back
jokes.setup: Baby won't stop crying for one person
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of baby preferring literally anyone else
jokes.explanation: Clean visual comedy with good timing and character-specific insecurity payoff

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Come on, Ben, it's okay. Daddy's here.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Here, let me try.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, he clearly wants me. We have a bond.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: *cries louder*

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, he stopped crying for me.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's... that's just a coincidence.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Give him to me.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: *immediately stops crying and smiles*

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?! How are you better at this than me?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I want to try!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: *reaches happily toward Phoebe*

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, my turn!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: *coos and laughs*

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm his father! What is happening?

jokes.text: You know, I once dated a Miss Cranky Pants. Lovely girl. Kind of moody.
jokes.setup: Monica calls Ben 'Mr. Cranky Pants'
jokes.punchline: Chandler treats it as literal name, adds ironic 'lovely girl' contradiction
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - takes innocent phrase literally, adds perfect contradiction. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Mr. Cranky Pants? That's a lovely girl.

jokes.text: Monica experiments with different positions/movements to stop Ben crying
jokes.setup: Trying to find the magic solution to stop crying
jokes.punchline: Visual of increasingly desperate physical attempts
jokes.explanation: Competent physical comedy but predictable baby-sitting scenario

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, there has to be something that stops the crying.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What if I just... hold my breath?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, that's not working. Um, what about pinching the bridge of my nose?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Still crying. Okay, okay, think Monica, think. What about... ice water on the face?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Nope! How about standing on my head?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous!

jokes.text: He hates me. My nephew hates me! What if my own baby hates me?
jokes.setup: Baby won't stop crying for Monica
jokes.punchline: Catastrophic leap to future maternal failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis - immediately jumps to worst-case scenario about hypothetical future

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? What is it? What's wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Maybe he's hungry.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just fed him!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: He might need to be burped.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I burped him! Oh my God, what if I'm a terrible mother? What if he knows? What if he's crying because he can sense that I'm going to mess him up? Oh God, he's going to be in therapy at 25 blaming me for everything!

jokes.text: You don't even have a boyfriend yet.
jokes.setup: Monica spiraling about future motherhood
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt reality check about current relationship status
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - cuts through melodrama with obvious truth. Good deflation technique.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Mon, you're freaking out about having kids someday, but you don't even have a boyfriend right now.

jokes.text: Joey, she does not look fat.
jokes.setup: Audience expects response to boyfriend comment
jokes.punchline: Chandler responds to completely different conversation
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - suggests ongoing parallel conversation we haven't heard. Creates confusion humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not a girl. And even if I was, I'd want a boyfriend who doesn't make me laugh at his own jokes.

jokes.text: Ross gagging and reacting badly to pie
jokes.setup: Ross eating what he thinks is key lime pie
jokes.punchline: Physical reaction reveals something's wrong
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the kiwi allergy plot. Functional physical comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mm, this is excellent key lime pie.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's not key lime pie.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?

jokes.text: Kiwi? Kiwi?! You said it was a key lime pie. No, I didn't. I said kiwi lime.
jokes.setup: Ross expects key lime pie
jokes.punchline: Monica's ridiculous 'kiwi lime' mishearing defense
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay setup, though Monica's defense is weak. The kiwi/key confusion works well.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, where's the key lime pie?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, I made kiwi lime instead.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Kiwi lime? Monica, the recipe said KEY lime.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know what I heard, Ross. Besides, kiwi lime is basically the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It is absolutely not the same thing!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, they're both green and they both have lime in the name.

jokes.text: Either that or my mouth is getting smaller!
jokes.setup: Monica asks if his tongue is swelling up
jokes.punchline: Ross's nasal voice delivers absurd alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Clever alternative perspective joke with good physical element (nasal voice). Economy and logic both strong.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, it's not swelling. I think I'm just getting a cold. My sinuses are really backed up.

jokes.text: Actually, it's getting better. Let's not go. Anyone up for Scrabble?
jokes.setup: Ross needs to go to hospital for shot
jokes.punchline: Sudden denial and random activity suggestion to avoid needles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's needle phobia, Scrabble suggestion adds nice specificity

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I don't need a shot. I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross, you need the shot.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, you know what? Let's go get coffee instead.

jokes.text: What? I have seven Catholic sisters. I've taken care of hundreds of kids.
jokes.setup: Doubt about Joey watching a baby
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific credential with mathematical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic - specific detail that doesn't add up mathematically but sounds convincing to him

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You can't watch the baby, Joey. You don't have any experience with kids.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Are you kidding? I'm great with kids. I've changed 200 diapers.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you've been an adult for 30 years.

jokes.text: I was gonna play basketball... but I guess that's out the window.
jokes.setup: Being asked to watch baby
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated martyrdom over minor schedule change
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm - treating minor inconvenience as major sacrifice

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh sure, I'd be happy to watch the baby. I mean, it's not like I have a job or a life or anything.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, you go ahead. I'll just cancel my very important plans of sitting on my couch in my underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm a giver. A martyr, really. They should make a statue of me. A sad, bitter statue.

jokes.text: My uncle Sal has a really big tongue. Is he the one with the beautiful wife?
jokes.setup: Joey mentions uncle's big tongue in context of Ross's swelling
jokes.punchline: Chandler's innocent question with unintended sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Subtle innuendo that works on multiple levels - innocent on surface but suggestive underneath

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My uncle had a tongue like that. It was huge.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So what happened to him?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, he couldn't talk, he couldn't eat. He had to leave the show.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: How long is it?

jokes.text: I'm in the shower and I'm writing a song, Stop me if you've heard it
jokes.setup: Phoebe introducing a new song
jokes.punchline: Impossibly literal song about the act of writing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdism - meta-commentary on songwriting process itself. Very original concept.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I wrote a song about writing a song. It's called 'Writing a Song.'

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm sitting here with my guitar, writing a song about sitting here with my guitar, writing a song.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: My fingers are on the strings, I'm thinking of words that rhyme with 'strings.' Now I'm singing the words that I'm thinking.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This song is about the song I'm writing. The song is happening right now, as I write it, in real time, in this song.

jokes.text: And Tegrin spelled backward is nirget
jokes.setup: Phoebe's literal shower song continues
jokes.punchline: Random shampoo brand observation with made-up profound conclusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur that sounds profound but is completely meaningless. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: It's that she's so bad she makes me want to put my finger through my eye into my brain and swirl it around.
jokes.setup: Terry explaining why he's firing Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific and violent metaphor for bad music
jokes.explanation: Extremely vivid and disturbing imagery that's both funny and memorable. Dark but effective.

jokes.text: Did you forget to pack the baby's anvil?
jokes.setup: Joey complaining they didn't bring enough stuff
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion of impossibly heavy/dangerous baby item
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm with absurd imagery. Maximum impact with minimal words.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We didn't bring enough stuff!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? We have diapers, formula, clothes, toys, books, a stroller, a car seat, a crib, a bassinet, a swing, a bouncer, a playpen, and a high chair. What else could we possibly need?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe a forklift?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A forklift. Because that's what every baby needs. A forklift. For what, exactly? Lifting their toys?

jokes.text: Aim him at that pack of babes. Maybe one of them will break away.
jokes.setup: Using baby to attract women
jokes.punchline: Military/hunting language applied to dating
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey pickup strategy with predatory language. Fits character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You see, the baby is like bait. Women see the baby, they come over, and boom—I move in for the kill.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's not a strategy, that's a war crime.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, all's fair in love and war. Speaking of which, I've locked onto target at two o'clock.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Please tell me you're not using Ben as a wingman.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I prefer to think of it as an offensive maneuver.

jokes.text: Well, don't think me immodest, but... me.
jokes.setup: Woman asks who the cute baby is
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks she's talking about him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey narcissism - completely misreads the situation in character-appropriate way

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Who's the cute baby?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, that's me!

jokes.text: I assume we're talking about the baby?
jokes.setup: Joey offers to let her smell 'him'
jokes.punchline: Woman's diplomatic clarification of obvious point
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Joey's weirdness. Clean and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: I think you misunderstood me. I meant your cologne.

jokes.text: My uterus just skipped a beat.
jokes.setup: Woman smelling baby's head
jokes.punchline: Biological response described in heart metaphor terms
jokes.explanation: Clever play on 'heart skipped a beat' with biological specificity. Memorable phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I love that smell! It's like... it's like my heart just exploded into a million pieces.

jokes.text: My brother and his boyfriend have been trying to adopt. What agency did you two go through?
jokes.setup: Woman impressed by Joey and Chandler with baby
jokes.punchline: Assumes they're a gay couple who adopted
jokes.explanation: Good setup for their discomfort. Logical assumption that creates awkward situation.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you two are so sweet! Are you guys... together?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? No, no, we're not—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're just friends.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Oh, that's so wonderful that you adopted! You must be such a great couple.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, we're not a couple.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This is actually my baby with my wife.

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Oh... well, you're both still amazing parents!

jokes.text: You can't just erase chalk.
jokes.setup: Phoebe arguing about being replaced at Central Perk
jokes.punchline: Treating erasable chalk as permanent commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - completely wrong about reality but delivered with conviction

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't believe they replaced me with someone who uses erasable chalk! That's not a commitment, that's a suggestion!

jokes.text: And, like, you know, a gold stick with a ball on top?
jokes.setup: Phoebe listing royal treatment for replacement musician
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the word 'scepter', describes it literally
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe vocabulary gap creating unintentionally childish description

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, if you're gonna replace me, the least you could do is give me the royal treatment. I'm talking about a nice hotel, room service, and one of those things... you know, that the king holds in his hand. It's like a stick, but fancier?

jokes.text: Terry's a jerk! That's why we always say, 'Terry's a jerk!' That's where that came from.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining why Terry is bad
jokes.punchline: Circular reasoning about the origin of their own phrase
jokes.explanation: Great circular logic joke with meta-commentary on catchphrase origins. Very Phoebe.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, Terry is bad because he does bad things. And we know he does bad things because he's bad.

jokes.text: You don't clean the cappuccino machine?
jokes.setup: Rachel offers to clean machine as bargain
jokes.punchline: Terry's shocked realization she's been neglecting basic duties
jokes.explanation: Good workplace comedy beat - reveals Rachel's poor work habits

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'll clean the machine if you want.

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: You'll clean it? When's the last time you cleaned anything?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I clean things!

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: Name one thing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I... well... I cleaned my apartment once.

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: Once? Rachel, you've lived here for five years!

jokes.text: I'm not some sloppy second, you know, charity band.
jokes.setup: Learning she won't be paid like the professional
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's hurt pride with unintentionally sexual terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with unintended double entendre adding extra layer

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't believe you're not gonna pay me like a professional!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I AM a professional! I've been doing this for years!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what? Fine. But I'm gonna need you to go down on me.

jokes.text: Maybe they can take the needle and squirt it into my mouth. Like a squirt gun.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to avoid needle injection
jokes.punchline: Childish alternative that's more complicated and weird
jokes.explanation: Good Ross neurosis with childish imagery. Shows his desperation to avoid needles.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not getting a shot. There's gotta be another way.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Well, there is an alternative...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes! Anything! I'll do anything!

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: You could swallow this pill... but you'd have to swallow it whole, and it's about the size of a golf ball.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A golf ball? That's insane! Just give me the shot.

jokes.text: Ross squeezing Monica's hand so hard she screams
jokes.setup: Ross getting injection, Monica offers hand for comfort
jokes.punchline: Role reversal where comforter becomes victim
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy reversal. Good escalation and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You can squeeze my hand if you need to.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, thanks.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! OW! Ross, you're breaking my hand!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You said I could squeeze it!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I didn't think you'd actually do it!

jokes.text: Next time we want to pick up women, we should go to the park and make out.
jokes.setup: Being mistaken for gay couple
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion to lean into the misperception
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm with absurd logical extension of their situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, maybe we should just lean into it. We could get matching outfits, hold hands in public, really commit to the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I'm not holding your hand!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Come on, it'll be fun. We could get a cute little apartment together, adopt a dog...

jokes.text: Can you tell him that? Because he thinks he looks too pink.
jokes.setup: Woman complimenting baby
jokes.punchline: Joey attributing adult vanity to infant
jokes.explanation: Good Joey misunderstanding of babies with projection of adult concerns

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: Oh, what a beautiful baby! He's so handsome!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, right? He's very vain about it too. This morning he spent twenty minutes looking at himself in the mirror.

jokes.text: We're two heterosexual guys hanging with the son of our other heterosexual friend, doing the usual straight-guy stuff.
jokes.setup: Trying to correct gay couple assumption
jokes.punchline: Overly defensive explanation that makes them sound more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Classic 'protesting too much' scenario. The over-explanation makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're not a couple! I mean, we're not together. I mean, we could be, theoretically, but we're not. Not that there's anything wrong with that! I mean, I'm not gay. Not that gay is bad! I'm just saying I'm not gay. We're just friends. Best friends. Who live together. And do everything together. And sometimes finish each other's... sandwiches.

jokes.text: We live in the building by the, uh, sidewalk.
jokes.setup: Asked where they live
jokes.punchline: Completely non-specific description that applies to every building
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey stupidity - thinks he's being specific but describes nothing. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: We live in a place with walls and a door.

jokes.text: BEN! BEN!
jokes.setup: Realizing they left baby on bus
jokes.punchline: Shouting baby's name in street as if he could respond
jokes.explanation: Functional panic comedy. The absurdity of shouting to a baby on a bus works.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: ROSS! ROSS!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: He's a baby, Joey. He's not gonna come running.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well what do you wanna do?!

jokes.text: That's good. Maybe he'll hear you and pull the cord!
jokes.setup: Joey shouting for baby on moving bus
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic suggestion that infant could stop the bus himself
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler sarcasm highlighting the absurdity of Joey's actions. Perfect economy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey, stop the bus! Stop the bus!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, because a baby can really reach the cord.

jokes.text: Sorry! I got my S's back! Celebrate. Celebrate...
jokes.setup: Ross's allergic reaction clearing up
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over pronunciation while trying to say 'celebrate'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's academic focus even during recovery

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Celebrate... celeb... no, it's celebrate. Cel-e-brate. Why is that so hard to say?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because you're having an allergic reaction and your face is swollen.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, I can say other words fine. It's just... cel... brate... See? See what I'm doing?

jokes.text: My name was on there, but now it just says carrot cake.
jokes.setup: Phoebe talking to replacement musician
jokes.punchline: Random menu item replaced her name on board
jokes.explanation: Odd detail that shows how thoroughly she's been erased. Mildly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Phoebe Buffay. I play guitar here.

dialogue.speaker: Replacement Musician
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, where's your name on the board?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It should be right there... oh my God, it says 'French Fries.'

jokes.text: Mine does.
jokes.setup: Stephanie saying her guitar doesn't have a strap
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's proud assertion of minor equipment advantage
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that show Phoebe's petty competitiveness beautifully

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, mine has a strap.

jokes.text: I was wondering what somebody might do if they left a baby on a city bus.
jokes.setup: Chandler calling transit authority
jokes.punchline: Transparently fake cover story about research
jokes.explanation: Standard panic comedy with weak cover story. Functional but not inspired.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hi, yes, I'm calling because I need to know if the subway runs all night? I'm doing research for a... a book I'm writing. It's about a man who takes the subway at 3 AM to meet his, uh, girlfriend. Who is not his wife.

jokes.text: Here's the deal: We lost a car seat on a bus. It's white plastic and it fits onto a stroller. Oh, and there was a baby in it.
jokes.setup: Joey taking over phone call
jokes.punchline: Treats baby as minor afterthought to lost equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and priority reversal. 'Oh, and there was a baby in it' is perfectly understated.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hello? Yeah, I'm looking for my stuff. Did you guys find a leather jacket, maybe some headshots? No? Well, I'm also looking for my baby.

jokes.text: Terry's a jerk and he won't let me work / And I hate Central Perk
jokes.setup: Phoebe performing despite being replaced
jokes.punchline: Singing complaints about current venue at current venue
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier Terry discussion, nice meta-commentary on current situation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: This place is a dump! The acoustics are terrible, the bathroom smells like a yak died in it, and the owner is a total cheapskate who won't even spring for decent lighting!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: But I'm gonna keep singing here anyway because apparently no one else wants to book me!

jokes.text: Actually, uh, ahem, we're both the father.
jokes.setup: Transit worker assumes one of them is the father
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked clarification makes situation much weirder
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy where attempt to clarify makes things much more confusing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no, no! I'm not the father! I can't even... I mean, we haven't even... It's not possible!

dialogue.speaker: Transit Worker
dialogue.line: Relax, buddy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, you don't understand. I'm infertile! I can't have children! I'm shooting blanks here!

jokes.text: That one has ducks on his T-shirt and this one has clowns. And Ben was definitely wearing ducks. Or clowns.
jokes.setup: Trying to identify the correct baby
jokes.punchline: Confident start dissolving into complete uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Good Joey confidence collapse. The either/or uncertainty is well-timed.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I know which one's mine. It's... it's the one with the... the face.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, they all have faces.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, it's the cute one.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: They're all cute. Why are they all so cute?!

jokes.text: That one's Ben! Remember he had that cute little mole by his mouth! / Okay, the mole came off.
jokes.setup: Chandler confidently identifying Ben by mole
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact announcement that identifying feature disappeared
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline with Joey's casual delivery of impossible situation. Good absurdist logic.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's Ben. I'd recognize that mole anywhere.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he had it removed.

jokes.text: Ducks is heads because ducks have heads.
jokes.setup: Need to assign heads/tails to duck vs. clown choice
jokes.punchline: Joey's bizarrely literal reasoning for arbitrary assignment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - sounds reasonable until you think about it. Clowns also have heads. Brilliant absurdist reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, heads is the duck, tails is the clown.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, no. Tails is the duck, because ducks have tails.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And heads is the clown, because... clowns have heads.

jokes.text: What kind of scary-ass clowns came to your birthday?
jokes.setup: Joey's logic about ducks having heads
jokes.punchline: Chandler's implication that Joey knew headless clowns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler response exposing the flaw in Joey's logic with dark humor

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ducks have heads.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, Joey, ducks have heads.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Then why do they always look so surprised?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't know. Why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because they just found out they have a head!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, how do you know about headless clowns?

jokes.text: $8.27. But not really, because I put in the first two.
jokes.setup: Phoebe showing her street performance earnings
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that she padded her own tip jar
jokes.explanation: Good character honesty moment. Shows Phoebe's integrity even when embarrassing.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Look how much I made today busking! Isn't that amazing?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I may have... put some of my own money in the tip jar to make it look fuller.

jokes.text: I got 25 cents and a condom.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining earnings from different songs
jokes.punchline: Bizarre non-monetary tip for 'Smelly Cat'
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and memorable detail. Condom as tip is both bizarre and slightly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I make money from 'Sticky Shoes' and 'The Song That Never Ends,' but you know what the weirdest tip I ever got for 'Smelly Cat'? A guy gave me a coupon for a free colonoscopy.

jokes.text: Did I accidentally drop a condom in your case? It's kind of an emergency.
jokes.setup: Previous condom tip joke
jokes.punchline: Man desperately retrieving his emergency contraception
jokes.explanation: Good callback that explains earlier joke while adding desperation element

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh no, my emergency condom! I threw it out!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's been in my wallet since high school!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, that thing's older than your last girlfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not funny! I need that condom!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh God, where's the trash? Where's the trash?!

jokes.text: Remember it? What do you think this is, a freckle?
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Monica remembers him jamming pencil in her hand
jokes.punchline: Monica showing permanent scar, revealing severity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ross was worse as a kid than expected. Physical evidence adds impact.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You remember when I jammed a pencil in your hand?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, yes! Look, I still have the scar.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, that's not the scar. That's just a line on your hand.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, this is the scar! See how dark it is?

jokes.text: They were infected. He wouldn't have made it.
jokes.setup: Monica discovering Ross cut legs off her Ken doll
jokes.punchline: Ross's serious medical justification for childhood vandalism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan justification treating toy doll like real patient. Perfect Ross paleontologist precision applied absurdly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ross! Why did you cut the legs off my Ken doll?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I was doing a surgical procedure. Ken had a compound fracture and I was attempting to perform an amputation to save his life.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's a doll, Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A doll in need of medical intervention, Monica. The appendages were clearly compromised.

jokes.text: How come it says, 'Property of Human Services'?
jokes.setup: Ross getting Ben back from Joey and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Discovery that they brought home the wrong baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that caps off the baby mix-up plot. Great timing and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Where's Ben? Where is he?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He's in the other room.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're gonna go get him now.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'go get him'? You don't know where he is?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, we kind of... we may have picked up the wrong baby.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: WHAT?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We went to the park, we came back with a baby, but now that I look at him...

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He's not ours.

jokes.text: Stay back! I've got kiwi! Run, Joey, run!
jokes.setup: Callback to Ross's kiwi allergy
jokes.punchline: Monica weaponizing fruit like it's dangerous
jokes.explanation: Good callback that treats harmless fruit like weapon. Action movie parody element.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! Is that a kiwi? Stay back! We need to get you to a hospital!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, I'm fine, I just—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! I'm calling an ambulance. Everyone, clear the apartment! We have a biohazard situation!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's just a kiwi.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Just a kiwi?! This fruit could kill him! I'm putting it in a hazmat container.

jokes.text: There is no 'top,' all right? That's the beauty of 'Smelly Cat.'
jokes.setup: Stephanie asking to start song from the top
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating terrible song like avant-garde masterpiece
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - treating her simple song like complex art. Great character delusion moment.

dialogue.speaker: Stephanie
dialogue.line: Can we start from the top?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: From the top? Oh, that was the top. That was the artistic peak of the song. It only gets weirder from here.

tmdb_overview: Chandler and Joey lose Ross's son, Ben, while checking out women on a bus. Phoebe loses her Central Perk singing gig to a professional singer. And Ross has an allergic reaction to Monica's kiwi-lime pie.
insight: Mid-season slump: 68.5 score driven by absurdist escalation, not character depth.
